reviews.review_description: The best game you can buy for little money
its amazing i had a super fun time its worth it

It has a lot of good things going for it, but some parts felt underbaked.
Find the second control room checkpoints too far apart.
Shooting sequence is nearly impossible because auto aim misses.

I usually use snap turning, but it didn't work well with this, so I made the vignette large and used smooth turning.

The cow eating gum was a bit weird.

I bought the game after experiencing the free trial and this is by far one of the most boring and most frustrating games I’ve ever played.
The story is stretched a mile long to the point it’s painfully boring.
The movement is insufferable, the little hover scooter is insanely slow and difficult to turn in most times.
The dialogue is lazy, and is a headache to listen to your daughter whine and complain about how men ruin everything as if it has anything to do with the story which there is lack of.
This game is also politically left and is crazy that the devs actually think Trump will still be relevant in the next 50 years which is where this game roughly takes place, I personally believe that real world politics have no reason to be in video games but devs don’t believe that honestly

Dealing with a disability, not being able to move my fingers, I would appreciate it big time, if you would update your controls. I need to toggle grab, to be able to play the game.
Will change my rating 🙂

reviews.review_title: I am so angry with this rubbish game
reviews.review_description: I played a trial and purchased this, launch the game it let's me watch thr first scene over and over and there is no game!! Nothing happens next I cannot believe it.

that being said, it doesn’t play fantastic… Some of the challenges are a little bit ridiculous given how Jenkee the control scheme is… I seen on the reviews, talking about the checkpoints and how you have to keep going back and doing the same stuff, and I agree with those people…

It’s an absolutely beautiful game visually speaking… The colors are incredible… The animations are clever, and the plot is really dystopian and interesting. I totally get that Jeff Bezos… I’m surprised he hasn’t Sued you lol also, you really get emotionally invested in the characters because it’s just a really clever storytelling and you buy into it without even knowing you’re buying into it! Fantastic job with the writing…

Also, the sound is fantastic… All of the environmental audio really transports you into this world that they’re trying to paint. This game is a feast for the eyes and the ears. 

still, I get to a point in the game, right around the race, where I just stop… I kind of lose interest because I have to keep going back and doing this thing that should be relatively easy if the control scheme were better. as a result, I’ve started and then stopped playing this game like six times now and I always stop in about the same spot just because well I really love it and want to see what happens next. It just seems like too much work you guys really need to look at the progression from the race on. I think a lot of people probably stop there and lose interest.

Still, I will keep trying. I will probably stop and restart it five or six more times and eventually I will progress past that point… But I don’t know if you really called that replay ability or just redundant.

graphics and colors 11 out of 10

Audio 10 out of 10

Character development 10 out of 10

Plot 10 out of 10

Playability four out of 10

Somebody message if you notice any update, where they fix the Jenkee controls… just in case I’ve given up again… Thanks

So I'll say what i like : Voice acting in general was good (the girl was like 10/10 her scream was to the bone) art style is nice comic style and the story had nice moment and the jokes are funny.... That for the good now for what i call bad: the gameplay most of the time boring (box transfer and "Stealth" which really not good) there some jokes out the place (not for the game)and the music ...boring and the story has bad moments as well and it short (3-4 hours) so buy it if it very cheap

Lemme tell you I was a little unsure about buying this game due to the worry of it not being long, but man this was not what I was expecting!! I was so into this game that I finished it in one play through and it still was awesome!!

Just finished it and: 
•A unique story plus perspective on growing technology 
•Amazing art style in graphics that I myself love 
•Humorous dialogue 
• More than one ending?! 
•EMOTIONAL ROLLERCOASTER (at least for me) 


Other stuff would be that certain levels were much harder than others but it honestly challenged me in a good way, besides it’s okay to be frustrated sometimes. Obviously some bugs in the timing of the dialogue and other things but I don’t usually notice them and It’s hey every game has bugs so I don’t mind. 

Ether way I say this game is crazy amazing and I loved the art style personally. YES PLAY THIS GAME ITS AWESOME. 😍😍😍😍

I went into this game thinking it was a short, simple puzzle adventure game! I WAS WRONG! But in a good way. This game is long and way more than a puzzle adventure! It has beautiful moments, twists, an actual story that drives you to follow it till the end! I 100% recommend!

reviews.review_title: Good Game held back by bugs
reviews.review_description:
The gameplay loop is a "Papers Please" style game.  You have a mundane job to perform while on the clock, and you have to make sure everything is following the rules. 
But you balance this with story segments interweaved throughout your shift and afterwards.

The gameplay is fun, the aesthetics are good, the voice acting is great, and the character dialogue is funny.

While this game has a lot of spirit, it unfortunately has a lot of bugs.
- I have snap turning enabled, but sometimes it changes back to smooth turning
- the Jüngle Gun has some weird interactions at times. Either not detecting a hit, or I'll grab a package and it shoves it backwards through a wall where I can't get. Sometimes I have to hold the trigger to hold a package, othertimes I have to tap it instead.
- god help you if you try to use the Jüngle Gun while moving.
- Interactable objects like levers or puzzle cubes will often clip through your cart, making it near impossible to turn them. But the game doesn't give you a way to move sideways or backwards, resulting in you having to drive in a circle to get a comfortable distance. Usually while you're on the clock.
- It seems like every story mission has at least one bug requiring a restart. i.e. A character tells you to the use the boost, but you can't for some reason. A door closes in front of you, trapping you in a hallway while other characters have a conversation on the other side. You need to do a timed puzzle which involves pointing the gun in certain directions but the game won't recognize it for some reason. You need to hide in an obvious open nook that looks just like the others you hide in, except there's an invisible wall so you can't. Etc.
- If you fail a story objective during a shift, choosing "Replay Shift" won't reactivate the objective. But "continue story" will!

It's frustrating because when everything works as intended, it's a fun game! But the bugs occur more often than not, which can ruin the experience.

reviews.review_title: One of the worst game’s I’ve ever played
reviews.review_description:
The Last Worker is one of the worst games I’ve ever played.

It has a quirky visual style and some good humor. That’s the end of the good parts.

Thematically it was offensive- another weak and unprincipled woke attack on Capitalism and business. Even in a video game someone wants to shove their agenda down your throat. 

The gameplay is brutal often requiring a re-do of sections not because it’s hard but because the controls are difficult to use and yet have to be executed flawlessly. So you wind up doing the exact same thing over 10, 20, 30 times mercilessly until you get it exactly right by sheer painful repetition. The best you can hope for is to incrementally work your way from checkpoint to checkpoint until the pain momentarily stops.

Gameplay is sometimes confusing because it isn’t clear what to do or how. The tutorial was pretty useless. Using the weapon effectively can often be frustrating. The movement is clunky and awkward mostly because the game has to allow for the movement of a full-sized hover chair. At times I was being prompted by the in-game characters to follow or “come here” but I couldn’t see where they were. By the end I just wanted it to end, especially when I was trying to remote control my robot buddy via a washed out white-tone television image over and over and over again.

This lacking development is magnified against many other better games available. But hey, what do you expect from a company that openly attacks productive genius and free trade? If you want to suffer for the humor and few good parts don’t pay full price. Otherwise, skip it. I wish I had.

Still cannot wrap my head around the mechanics.
Search YouTube for Paradise Decay The Last Worker to see my 1st Impressions video of the game.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 4 stars! We watched your video - be careful on checking the boxes, the one at 35:39 wasn't a weight issues, but was damaged (you can see bits of damage on the box) :)

reviews.review_description: I can't find the right words to describe the beauty of this title because I'm convinced that it wouldn't do it justice.  Personally, I am convinced that he deserves GOTY 2023, I hope so, with all my heart

Brilliant game play, funny, serious and everything in between! Part story part game. 
Cleverly done and amazing cartoon borderlands style graphics

developer_response.body: Thanks for dropping 5 Stars! Glad you enjoyed

reviews.review_description: You can tell there was effort put into this game truly. However there are countless plot holes and game breaking bugs that they don’t bother to fix. ie the remote controlling skew chapter where it says to press a button and literally nothing happens. Fix your  game and maybe you’ll get a better rating.

This was a surprise purchase for me and so far I’m really enjoying it. I’ve played for a few hours and there are some weird moments where the sound skips a little or I’m not sure what to do but overall it’s a fun game and I’m enjoying myself.

If you are on the fence about it use my code to get 25% off tiny.cc/hdt5vz and give it a shot!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5 stars - we've not had any reports on the sound dropping, but if you could let us know via the Wired Productions Discord, or via WiredPro on Facebook, we'll look into it

reviews.review_description: Very funny game, I'm loving it so far.  The gameplay is good, your moving items around this huge center.  Lots of multitasking skill needed.  You have work to get a passing grade and move onto the next shift.   It's very comfortable to play sitting.  If you need 25% off: vr.miketunes.com/last

The last worker is an awesome game, and very long for its price. I love the whole idea, but I wish there was an “infinite” mode that allows you to do the packages day by day as if you were in the company, and maybe skew would like say things to you idk. 

A few things I wish where different:

1. The medical area
This area I found extremely difficult. It would be nice to maybe have like a bit of help if you fail it to many times because I failed it like 26 times.

2. The endings (spoilers)
These feel a little inconclusive, like I understand the ending where hoverbird explodes as like a way to just stop the chaos, but they didn’t show what happened afterwards, I mean did the robots just shut down afterwards? 
The ending where you become the ceo needs more, I can’t really think of what but it just feels kind of like a missed opportunity.
The home ending is okay but it would be nice to show like maybe them trying to get skew out of the tree, and maybe Kurt and Cheyenne do something together.

I mean these addons would make a good DLC or maybe just part of the whole game.

Overall: 8.8/10

I did 5 stars because this is a smaller game and it could use the support.

Great game, but needs a few minor changes.

reviews.review_title: I will update this after more play
reviews.review_description:
The tutorial was super confusing. I couldn't understand why the controls on my vehicle wouldn't work. It took me so long to realize that the controls are just for show, even though my hands can grab them. I have to push buttons on my Oculus controllers in order to move anything. It would be so much more fun if the controls on the vehicle did something.

The game play itself is kind of boring so far, but hopefully it will become more interesting than just finding a box and tossing a box and finding a box and tossing a box.

Update: do you think I didn't try moving the controls? They don't do anything, they don't even move. I checked the settings in case it was something there, but couldn't find anything. So even if it's just mine that's broken, it's broken. I have to use my hand controls and the things attached to the vehicle do nothing.

I've gotten further into the game and it's fun exploring the little tunnels and playing the little puzzles, but they're pretty basic and relatively easy. I can't tell you how long it took me to figure out that that bubble gum shooter (tracker) was on a rack over my head. I don't know how I was supposed to know that. I had to restart that chapter several times cuz I thought my gun was glitching.

The settings page is completely unintuitive. I have no idea what half of the terminology means. I'd have to play with it, then go back into the game and see if things look different, and if it's more uncomfortable, I'd have to go back to settings and see if I can remember what I just did. It would be so much easier if you just explained it in layman terms.

I'm disappointed that this is a sitting game. I know I can stand while I play, but you don't move around at all other than your hands.

The graphics are interesting and the voice artists do a nice job. I think I'm keeping it three stars I think it's nothing spectacular, but for the price it's worth it.

developer_response.body: Hey Heryllion - you can absolutely play with the vehicle (or JünglePod) controls, for true VR immersion :) Just reach down to each stick, Clench the trigger to grab and give it a wiggle! Yep, there is far more than tossing boxes and we hope you come back to review once you've completed the game - enjoy!
